pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of BWS ESG Credits solution for financial institutions to add ESG impact reporting to digital channels. Designed for banks, investment advisors, venture capitalists, and issuers to track and report green asset investments with blockchain verification.
useCases: Banks providing investor reports for green assets to customers
Institutional investors viewing portfolio reports of green assets
Venture capitalists providing sponsors with project-level impact reports
Issuers publishing impact details of green assets for promotional use
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured impact data
Store ESG framework profiles immutably on blockchain
Issue blockchain certificates for customer green investments
Prevent double-counting of green project benefits
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right Solution Overview ESG Credits API chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: FAN Game Cube | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: FAN Game Cube is a B2B2C platform enabling sports clubs to create new revenue streams through NFT-based fan engagement. Fans can participate in multiple sports, tournaments, and seasons through gamified experiences tied to real matches.
useCases: Sports clubs generating new revenue streams
Fans playing virtual games during live matches
Weekend chess tournaments for fan engagement
Soccer VIP finals with NFT rewards
Cricket yearly season campaigns
Multi-sport fan experiences

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Calendar API operations for creating, listing, and managing scheduled game events or tournaments. Calendars represent time-bound events linked to specific fields, supporting single matches or entire seasons.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for a sports season or tournament
Response returns calendarId for linking matches and games

useCases: Create season-long calendars like LaLiga 2025-2026
Schedule single event tournaments like UEFA Champions League Final
Manage multiple matches within a calendar period
Link time-bound gameplay to specific fields
Organize fan campaigns by season or event

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Use new_calendar operation with name parameter (e.g., 'LaLiga 2025-26') to create a scheduled event timeline

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Calendar ID
implementationSteps.description: The API returns calendarId which anchors all matches and gameplay within that time period

implementationSteps.title: List or Delete
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars to view all calendars or delete_calendar to remove a calendar by calendarId

pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Cubes API for managing NFT zones within a Field. Cubes are automatically generated when creating fields, represent unique NFTs owned by fans, and track ownership, rewards, and live event impacts.

codeExamples.description: Lists cubes for a field with pagination support
Returns cube data with coordinates and pagination key

useCases: List all NFT zones within a sports field
Update pricing for specific cubes
Track cube ownership by fans
Map game events to cube coordinates
Enable rewards for cube owners based on live events

implementationSteps.title: Cubes Auto-Generation
implementationSteps.description: Cubes are automatically created when a new field is created, tokenizing the field into interactive zones

implementationSteps.title: List Cubes with Pagination
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes with fieldId and limit parameters; use lastEvaluatedKey for pagination through large cube sets

implementationSteps.title: Update Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList (using i_j format), and priceInCents to set cube prices

Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. triangle-exclamation Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. hashtag list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. hashtag list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); hashtag list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). hashtag update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) hashtag update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Event Types API for defining in-game occurrences like goals, passes, or assists. Events are registered with spatial coordinates and affect cube owners whose zones match action locations with assigned point values.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new event type with 100 points for passes
Returns eventTypeId for registering live events

useCases: Define scoring system for different game events
Assign points for goals, passes, assists in soccer
Create custom event types for different sports
Update point values for event types
Link live events to reward calculations

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Type
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type with fieldId, eventTypeName (e.g., 'pass', 'goal'), and eventTypePoints to define scoring

implementationSteps.title: List Available Events
implementationSteps.description: Call list_event-types with fieldId to retrieve all event types for a field

implementationSteps.title: Update or Delete Events
implementationSteps.description: Use update_event-type to change point values or delete_event-type to remove event types

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Field API for creating virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive cube zones. Fields serve as the foundational canvas for gameplay, linked to real-world stadiums and matches with configurable dimensions.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new football field with specified dimensions in centimeters
Returns fieldId and automatically creates all cubes for the field

useCases: Create virtual replicas of real stadiums like Camp Nou
Tokenize basketball courts like Madison Square Garden
Define field dimensions for different sports
Automatically generate cube zones for fan ownership
Link fields to live match events

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field with name, sport, fieldLength, fieldWidth (in cm), and cubeLength to define the virtual stadium

implementationSteps.title: Automatic Cube Generation
implementationSteps.description: All cubes are automatically created based on field dimensions and cube size when the field is created

implementationSteps.title: Manage Fields
implementationSteps.description: Use list_fields to view all fields or delete_field to remove a field (note: deleting a field removes all associated cubes)

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden hashtag new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) hashtag new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. hashtag list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters hashtag list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id hashtag list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. hashtag delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. hashtag delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js hashtag delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. circle-info All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Live Events API for recording in-match events at specific field coordinates during matches. Events like goals or plays are captured and mapped to corresponding cubes for scoring and rewards processing.

codeExamples.description: Records a live event at specific x,y coordinates during a match
Returns success status after event is recorded and mapped to cubes

useCases: Record live goals during soccer matches
Track player movements and passes in real-time
Capture chess moves during tournaments
Map game events to cube owners for rewards
Integrate AI-driven event detection systems
Manually log events via organizer dashboard
Enable automatic scoring based on live gameplay

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Match Context
implementationSteps.description: Ensure matchId, fieldId, and calendarId are available for the scheduled game

implementationSteps.title: Record Event with Coordinates
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event with x,y coordinates where the event occurred and the eventTypeId defining the event type

implementationSteps.title: Automatic Processing
implementationSteps.description: The API maps the event to corresponding cubes based on coordinates and processes scoring/rewards for cube owners

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Live Events LiveEvent is the base API object used to record an in-match event that occurs at a specific coordinate ( x , y ) on a Field during a scheduled Match ( Calendar ). It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. hashtag new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter hashtag new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. hashtag new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. hashtag new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js hashtag new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Match API operations for creating time-bound gameplay sessions tied to specific fields. Covers creating, updating, listing, and deleting matches with support for team names, flags, status tracking, and IPFS image storage. Matches represent game instances like football matches or chess rounds scheduled via Calendar objects.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match with team details, start time, and promotional image for PSG vs Man City
Updates only the description field of an existing match
Response showing match details with IPFS image URL after listing matches

useCases: Create football match sessions with team information and scheduling
Track chess tournament rounds with player details
Schedule basketball games with status tracking (scheduled, playing, finished)
Update match information and pricing during event lifecycle
Store match promotional images on IPFS for decentralized access

implementationSteps.title: Create Match
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, match name, team details, and base64 encoded images. Returns matchId for reference.

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Status
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match with matchId and calendarId to modify fields like status (scheduled/playing/finished/canceled), description, or pricing.

implementationSteps.title: List and Monitor
implementationSteps.description: Query matches using list_matches with calendarId to retrieve all matches or specific match details with IPFS image URLs.

It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura circle-info Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. hashtag new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). hashtag new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js hashtag new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. hashtag update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag update_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) hashtag update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js hashtag update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. hashtag list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. hashtag list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js hashtag list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. hashtag delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. hashtag delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js hashtag delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Plays (Field-Calendar) relationship API that models the temporal lifecycle of fields in NFT Game Cube. A Field defines static spatial configuration while Calendar entries represent dynamic matches or tournaments. Includes operations for creating, listing, and deleting field-calendar relationships with IPFS image support.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new field-calendar relationship for PSG 2025-26 season with descriptive image
Response showing created field-calendar with IPFS image URL
Lists all calendars associated with a specific field ID

useCases: Link PSG Champions League 2026 calendar to stadium field configuration
Create New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season calendar for basketball court
Manage multiple tournament calendars on single sports field
Track seasonal schedules with field-specific configurations
Store season promotional images on IPFS with field associations

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Link
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field-calendar with fieldId, calendarId, description (e.g., 'PSG 2025-26'), and optional base64 image to establish relationship.

implementationSteps.title: Query Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Call list_field-calendars with fieldId to retrieve all calendar relationships for that field with IPFS URLs.

implementationSteps.title: Remove Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Delete field-calendar link using delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId parameters.

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Prize API for managing reward assets in NFT Game Cube matches. Prizes represent rewards based on cube performance including digital NFTs, tokens, physical goods, or experiences. Supports creating, listing, updating, and deleting prizes with positions (1st, 2nd, 3rd) and HTML descriptions stored on IPFS.

codeExamples.description: Creates first place prize with signed merchandise for match winner
Response showing prize details with IPFS snapshot URL
Updates prize with HTML formatted description for rich display

useCases: Award signed merchandise to top performing cube owners
Distribute VIP match tickets as second place prizes
Offer Zoom calls with athletes for third place winners
Grant bonus BWS tokens to top leaderboard positions
Provide physical goods or experiences based on cube performance

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1-3), prize name, description, and snapshot image. System returns prizeId.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Prize Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match-prize to add HTML descriptions and additional images (image1, image2, image3) for rich prize presentation.

implementationSteps.title: Manage Prize Catalog
implementationSteps.description: List all prizes for a match using list_match-prizes or remove prizes with delete_match-prize using prizeId.

It can be a digital NFT, a token bonus, a physical good, or an experience, configurable by the solution owner to drive fan engagement and incentivize Cube ownership. pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive overview of FAN Game Cube platform that revolutionizes fan engagement through NFT-based digital field ownership. Explains how sports enthusiasts purchase cubes representing field sections, earn points from real-time events, and unlock rewards. Covers multi-sport support, pricing strategies, revenue potential, and one-click purchase integration.
useCases: Football stadiums divided into 500-2000 cubes with premium penalty box pricing
Chess tournaments tokenizing 64 chessboard squares for fan interaction
Basketball courts with cube allocation for three-point arc and paint zone
Cricket fields dividing batting zones and boundary sections for engagement
Tennis courts tokenizing baseline and service boxes for match participation

implementationSteps.title: Define Cube Allocation
implementationSteps.description: Determine number of cubes and field division strategy based on sport (e.g., 500-2000 for football, 64 for chess). Assign premium pricing to strategic areas.

implementationSteps.title: Configure Pricing Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Set prices for prime field areas (higher rates) and secondary zones (lower rates like $10). Calculate revenue potential: cubes × average price = total income.

implementationSteps.title: Deploy Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Integrate one-click JavaScript widget on club website for credit card purchases. Widget enables frictionless transactions without complex gateways.

implementationSteps.title: Launch Fan Engagement
implementationSteps.description: Fans purchase cubes, manage collections via mobile app, track live matches, and compete on leaderboards for prizes and rewards.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here arrow-up-right . hashtag Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. hashtag NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. hashtag Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. hashtag Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. hashtag Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. hashtag How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. hashtag Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. hashtag Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube chevron-left Next FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Cube Allocation for Different Sports NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading Pricing and Monetization Strategy Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget How Fans Can Participate and Win Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics Why Choose FAN Game Cube? pages.title: X Bot | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview page for X Bot, a Telegram-based analytics platform providing accurate X (Twitter) engagement metrics using official X API. Documents 73 commands across 12 categories including filtering, configuration, raid coordination, analytics reporting, and administration. Serves as navigation hub for complete command reference.
useCases: Track X (Twitter) account engagement metrics using official API
Coordinate community engagement raids on X posts
Generate automated performance reports and leaderboards
Monitor keywords, cashtags, and mentions across tracked accounts
Customize scoring systems for likes, retweets, replies, and views

implementationSteps.title: Initialize Bot
implementationSteps.description: Run /start command to set up the bot in your Telegram group and receive welcome instructions.

implementationSteps.title: Add Tracking
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts to specify X (Twitter) accounts to monitor for engagement metrics and analytics.

implementationSteps.title: Generate Report
implementationSteps.description: Execute /recreate command to fetch latest X data and generate analytics reports with leaderboards.

Perform daily operations like viewing reports and starting raids

implementationSteps.title: First Time Setup
implementationSteps.description: Execute /start to initialize, /add_accounts to track X accounts, /set_project_name to configure identity, and /recreate to generate first report.

implementationSteps.title: Daily Operations
implementationSteps.description: Use /report for latest analytics, /raidx to start engagement raids, and /raid_status to monitor active raid progress.

implementationSteps.title: Advanced Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Apply /set_x_filtering for complex tracking, /set_schedule for automation, and /set_points for custom engagement scoring.

Initialize the bot for your group /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Add X accounts to track /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Configure project name /recreate arrow-up-right - Generate your first analytics report hashtag Daily Operations /report arrow-up-right - View latest performance analytics /raidx arrow-up-right - Start a new engagement raid /raid_status arrow-up-right - Check active raid progress hashtag Advanced Configuration /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Create named filters for complex tracking /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Automate report generation /set_points arrow-up-right - Customize engagement scoring hashtag All Commands by Category hashtag Filter Setup (20 commands) Manage tracking filters for accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignore lists. Commands: /list_filters arrow-up-right - Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each. /show_filter arrow-up-right - Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags,. /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. /add_keywords arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. /add_cashtags arrow-up-right - Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. /add_mentions arrow-up-right - Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. /add_excludes arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. /add_ignore arrow-up-right - Adds X accounts to ignore. /remove_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes specific X accounts from tracking. /remove_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. /remove_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes specific cashtags from tracking. /remove_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes X handles from mention tracking. /remove_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes keywords from the exclusion list. /remove_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes X accounts from the ignore list. /clear_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. /clear_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes ALL keywords from the filter. /clear_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. /clear_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes ALL mentions from the filter. /clear_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. /clear_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. hashtag Basic Commands (4 commands) Essential bot operations including help, initialization, and status checks. Commands: /help arrow-up-right - Displays a complete list of available bot commands and their descriptions. /start arrow-up-right - Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. /status arrow-up-right - Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. /get_chatid arrow-up-right - Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. hashtag Advanced Filtering (3 commands) Create named filters for complex tracking scenarios and multi-project management. Commands: /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. /get_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. /delete_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. hashtag Project Configuration (10 commands) Customize project metadata including name, description, logo, and URLs for reports. Commands: /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the. /get_project_name arrow-up-right - Displays the current project name configured for your community. /set_project_description arrow-up-right - Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. /get_project_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current project description configured for your community. /set_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. /get_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. /set_project_logo arrow-up-right - Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. /get_project_logo arrow-up-right - Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. /set_project_urls arrow-up-right - Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. /get_project_urls arrow-up-right - Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. hashtag Raid Commands (11 commands) Coordinate X (Twitter) engagement raids to amplify post visibility and reach. Commands: /raidx arrow-up-right - Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. /raid_status arrow-up-right - Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. /raid_stop arrow-up-right - Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. /raid_history arrow-up-right - Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. /set_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior,. /get_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration,. /set_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. /get_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. /set_raid_image arrow-up-right - Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. /auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. /get_auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after. hashtag Schedule Management (3 commands) Configure automated report generation schedules with custom intervals. Commands: /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. /get_schedule arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. /delete_schedule arrow-up-right - Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. hashtag Calendar & Time (2 commands) Manage calendar settings and time periods for report generation cycles. Commands: /set_calendar arrow-up-right - Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. /get_calendar arrow-up-right - Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often. hashtag Reports (2 commands) Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Commands: /report arrow-up-right - Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report for your community. /recreate arrow-up-right - Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report by fetching latest X (Twitter) data and creating new leaderboards. hashtag Leaderboard Customization (10 commands) Customize scoring points, colors, titles, and display settings for leaderboard reports. Commands: /set_points arrow-up-right - Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_points arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics. /set_colors arrow-up-right - Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_colors arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics. /set_top_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. /set_best_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. /set_engagement_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. /get_titles arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including. /set_top_count arrow-up-right - Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. /get_top_count arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report. hashtag Topic Management (2 commands) Configure Telegram topic/thread settings for bot operation. Commands: /set_topic arrow-up-right - Configures which forum topic (thread) the bot should send reports to in Telegram groups that use forum-style topics. /get_topic arrow-up-right - Displays which forum topic (thread) is currently configured to receive bot reports. hashtag Admin (1 commands) Administrative functions for managing bot notifications and permissions. Commands: /add_admin arrow-up-right - Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as. hashtag Subscription & Credits (5 commands) Manage billing, subscriptions, credits, and usage tracking. Commands: /subscription arrow-up-right - Provides access to Stripe customer portal for subscription management including billing history,. /buy arrow-up-right - Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription. /get_credits arrow-up-right - Displays the current credit usage and availability for the bot. /payment_success arrow-up-right - Handles successful payment confirmation after Stripe checkout completion. /payment_cancelled arrow-up-right - Handles payment cancellation when users exit Stripe checkout without completing payment. pages.title: Admin | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the /add_admin command for designating specific group administrators to receive bot notifications. Allows limiting notification recipients to reduce alert noise, sending only to admins who manage the bot. All designated users must already be group administrators for credit warnings and system alerts.

codeExamples.description: Designates three specific administrators to receive bot notifications about credits, subscriptions, and errors
Clears designated admin list, reverting to default behavior of notifying all group admins

useCases: Designate bot manager to receive credit exhaustion warnings
Limit subscription renewal alerts to billing administrator
Reduce notification noise for non-bot managing admins
Configure specific admins for error alert monitoring

pages.title: Advanced Filtering | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Advanced Filtering commands including /set_x_filtering, /get_x_filtering, and /delete_x_filtering. Enables creating sophisticated named filters with complex X API query syntax using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:) for multi-condition tracking scenarios.

codeExamples.description: Creates named filter tracking mentions of project account or project token hashtag
Creates filter tracking posts from specific influencers containing 'BWS' keyword
Creates filter for trending hashtags while excluding scam content and ignoring spam accounts

useCases: Track multiple KOL accounts mentioning project keywords
Monitor hashtag combinations with exclusion rules
Create complex filters combining mentions, keywords, and account filters
Manage multiple named filters for different tracking scenarios
Delete specific filters or clear all advanced filters at once

implementationSteps.title: Create Named Filter
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_x_filtering with filter name and X API query syntax combining operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, mention:, keywords:, exclude:, ignore:).

implementationSteps.title: Review Filters
implementationSteps.description: Execute /get_x_filtering to list all named filters with their complete query syntax for verification.

implementationSteps.title: Remove Filters
implementationSteps.description: Delete specific filter by name or use wildcard '*' with /delete_x_filtering to remove all advanced filters.

Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:). Use this for sophisticated tracking scenarios requiring multiple conditions and Boolean logic that go beyond simple add/remove commands. Admin Only Copy /set_x_filtering project_mentions mention:@MyProject OR #MyProjectToken Copy /set_x_filtering my_kols from:@CryptoHayes OR from:@IncomeSharks keywords:BWS Copy /set_x_filtering trending #DeFi OR #crypto OR #blockchain exclude:scam ignore:@SpamBot Parameters: Name Type Example filter_name required my_filter query_syntax required value hashtag /get_x_filtering Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. Shows each filter's name and its complete X API query syntax, helping you understand what content is being tracked. Use this to review your sophisticated filtering setup created with /set_x_filtering. Admin Only Copy /get_x_filtering hashtag /delete_x_filtering Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Remove a specific filter by name to stop tracking that particular query, or use the wildcard "*" to delete all advanced filters at once. This does not affect simple filters managed with add/remove commands. pages.title: Basic Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents basic bot commands including /help, /start, /status, and /get_chatid. These essential operations cover initialization, command reference display, operational status checks, and retrieving Telegram group chat IDs. Admin-only commands show different permissions levels between administrators and regular users.

codeExamples.description: Displays complete list of available commands with descriptions, showing admin commands to administrators and limited commands to regular users
Initializes bot in group chats or shows welcome message with 'Add to Group' button in private messages
Verifies bot is operational and provides documentation link for setup instructions

useCases: Initialize bot after adding to new Telegram group
Check bot responsiveness after installation
Retrieve group chat ID for technical integrations
Display available commands based on user permission level
Verify bot operational status for troubleshooting

implementationSteps.title: Add Bot to Group
implementationSteps.description: Use /start in private message to get 'Add to Group' button, then add bot to your Telegram group.

implementationSteps.title: Verify Installation
implementationSteps.description: Run /status in group to confirm bot is active and responsive with access to documentation.

implementationSteps.title: Explore Commands
implementationSteps.description: Execute /help to see all available commands based on your admin status and get command descriptions.

Group administrators see all commands including advanced configuration options, while regular users see a limited list of basic commands they can use. Use this to discover what the bot can do and get quick command references. Admin Only Copy /help hashtag /start Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. In group chats, confirms the bot is running and directs you to use /help for available commands. In private messages with the bot, shows a welcome message with an "Add to Group" button to install the bot in your Telegram group. Also handles payment confirmations when you complete a subscription purchase. Admin Only Copy /start hashtag /status Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. Admin Only Copy /status hashtag /get_chatid Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. This identifier is useful for technical configurations, integrations with other systems, or when contacting support. The chat ID is a unique number that Telegram assigns to each group and is required for certain advanced features and API integrations. pages.title: Calendar & Time | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Calendar & Time commands /set_calendar and /get_calendar for managing recurring report generation schedules. Allows setting start dates and cadence intervals for automatic report creation. All times use UTC timezone with support for past dates up to 6 days and any future dates.

codeExamples.description: Sets up weekly automatic report generation starting June 20, 2025, creating reports every 7 days
Displays current calendar configuration showing start date and report generation cadence in UTC timezone

useCases: Schedule weekly reports starting from specific date
Configure daily automated report generation
Set up monthly reporting cadence for community analytics
Verify current report generation schedule
Adjust report frequency by updating calendar settings

implementationSteps.title: Configure Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_calendar with start date (DD/MM/YYYY format) and cadence (e.g., 7days) to establish recurring report schedule in UTC.

implementationSteps.title: Verify Schedule
implementationSteps.description: Run /get_calendar to confirm start date and report generation frequency are configured correctly.

Specify a start date and how often reports should be generated (cadence in days). The bot will automatically create reports at the specified intervals starting from the chosen date. pages.title: Filter Setup | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents all filter setup commands for X Bot, including commands to view, add, remove, and clear filters for tracking X (Twitter) accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. These filters determine which X content is monitored and included in reports and leaderboards.

codeExamples.description: Adds multiple X (Twitter) accounts to track for monitoring posts and engagement
Adds keywords and hashtags to track, using quotes for multi-word phrases
Adds exclusion keywords to filter out unwanted content from tracking

useCases: Track posts from specific crypto influencers and team members
Monitor cryptocurrency token mentions using cashtags like $BTC or $ETH
Filter out spam and bot accounts from community engagement reports
Organize different tracking groups using named filters (team, KOLs, influencers)
Track brand mentions and project-related keywords across X (Twitter)
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Filter Setup hashtag View Filters hashtag /list_filters Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup. Admin Only Copy /list_filters hashtag /show_filter Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking. Admin Only Copy /show_filter Copy /show_filter filter=kols Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag Add Filters hashtag /add_accounts Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors). Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @CryptoHayes filter optional team hashtag /add_keywords Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required crypto filter optional trending hashtag /add_cashtags Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $BWS filter optional tokens hashtag /add_mentions Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /add_excludes Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /add_ignore Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @SpamBot filter optional clean hashtag Remove/Clear Filters hashtag /remove_accounts Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @OldAccount filter optional kols hashtag /remove_keywords Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required oldterm filter optional trending hashtag /remove_cashtags Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $OLDTOKEN filter optional tokens hashtag /remove_mentions Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /remove_excludes Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /remove_ignore Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @ReinstatedAccount filter optional clean hashtag /clear_accounts Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag /clear_keywords Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional trending hashtag /clear_cashtags Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional tokens hashtag /clear_mentions Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional influencers hashtag /clear_excludes Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional clean hashtag /clear_ignore Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional clean Previous X Bot Commands chevron-left Next Basic Commands chevron-right Last updated 11 days ago Was this helpful? paragraphs: Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup.

Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking.

Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors).

Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project.

Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols.

Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts.

Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking.

Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track.

Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project.
Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases.
Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored.
Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored.
Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out.
Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria.
Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list.
Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking.
Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking.
Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking.
Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked.
Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked.

pages.title: Leaderboard Customization | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents leaderboard customization commands for X Bot, including setting point multipliers for different engagement types (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, views), customizing report colors, configuring section titles, and controlling the number of users displayed in leaderboards. These settings personalize how performance metrics are calculated and displayed.

codeExamples.description: Sets scoring multipliers for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and views to calculate leaderboard points
Configures HTML hex colors for displaying engagement metrics in visual reports
Sets custom title text and color for the main leaderboard section

useCases: Customize engagement scoring to prioritize replies over likes for community discussions
Brand leaderboard reports with project-specific colors matching visual identity
Configure different point weights to value high-effort engagement (quotes, replies) more than passive engagement (likes)
Set custom titles for weekly or monthly competition leaderboards
Control leaderboard size to highlight top 5, 10, or 20 performers
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Leaderboard Customization hashtag /set_points Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. These multipliers determine how many points each type of engagement contributes to a user's total score. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Copy /set_points 5 1 20 10 0.01 Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_points Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used. Copy /get_points hashtag /set_colors Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_colors Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set. hashtag /set_top_title Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_best_title Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above the highlighted best tweet. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_engagement_title Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /get_titles Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section. hashtag /set_top_count Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. This controls how many top-performing users are shown in the generated reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example count required value hashtag /get_top_count Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report leaderboard. Shows either the configured value or indicates default value (10) is being used.
