pages.title: Project Configuration | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents project configuration commands for X Bot, including setting project name, descriptions (short and long), uploading logos, and configuring project URLs. These settings customize how the project appears in reports and on the public xbot.ninja website dashboard.

codeExamples.description: Sets the project name that appears in all generated reports and on the website dashboard

codeExamples.code: /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance
codeExamples.description: Sets a short 1-2 sentence description of the project for reports
Configures project website and social media links for the dashboard

useCases: Brand analytics reports with official project name instead of generic Telegram group name
Create public project dashboard on xbot.ninja for investor discovery
Upload project logo to appear prominently in all generated reports
Provide project description and links for potential investors viewing the dashboard
Customize report identity when sharing performance data with partners or community
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Project Configuration hashtag /set_project_name Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the website dashboard. This is the main identifier for your community and should be your project's official name or brand. Use this to customize reports with your project's identity instead of using generic Telegram group names. Admin Only Copy /set_project_name CryptoDAO Community Copy /set_project_name My Awesome Project Parameters: Name Type Example project_name required My Project hashtag /get_project_name Displays the current project name configured for your community. Use this to verify what name will appear in reports and on the website, or to check if a project name has been set yet. Admin Only Copy /get_project_name hashtag /set_project_description Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. This should be a concise summary of what your project does or your community's mission (1-2 sentences). Use this to provide context about your project's goals when sharing reports with investors, partners, or community members. Admin Only Copy /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance Parameters: Name Type Example description required value hashtag /get_project_description Displays the current project description configured for your community. Use this to review the short description that appears in reports, or to check if a description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_long_description Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. Unlike the short description, this can be multiple paragraphs and include more in-depth information about your project's mission, features, roadmap, and team. This appears on your public dashboard page to provide visitors with complete project details. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example long_description required value hashtag /get_project_long_description Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. Use this to review the comprehensive description that visitors see on your public dashboard page, or to check if a long description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_logo Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. Send your logo as a photo or file (PNG, JPG, or GIF recommended). The logo should be square or rectangular and will be displayed prominently in all generated reports and on your public dashboard page. Admin Only hashtag /get_project_logo Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. Use this to verify which logo will appear in reports and on the website, or to share the logo URL with team members. If no logo is set, you'll receive a message prompting you to upload one with /set_project_logo. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_urls Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. These URLs appear on the website dashboard as clickable links, making it easy for visitors to connect with your project across different platforms. You can include your website, Twitter/X, Discord, Telegram, GitHub, documentation, or any other relevant links. Separate multiple URLs with spaces or commas. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example urls required https://myproject.com hashtag /get_project_urls Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. Use this to review which links appear on the website dashboard, verify URLs are correct, or check if any URLs have been set. pages.title: Raid Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents raid commands for X Bot, enabling coordinated community engagement campaigns on specific X (Twitter) posts. Includes commands to launch raids with target metrics, track real-time progress, manage raid history, configure defaults, and set up automatic raids for top-performing content.

codeExamples.description: Launches a raid on a specific X post with target engagement goals for likes and retweets
Configures default settings for all future raids including targets and duration
Enables automatic raids on the best tweet of the day with specified targets

useCases: Coordinate community to boost important project announcements on X (Twitter)
Support partner projects by organizing engagement raids on their posts
Run community competitions with raid targets for prizes or rewards
Automatically amplify daily top-performing content with scheduled raids
Track raid performance and community participation rates over time
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Raid Commands hashtag /raidx Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements. Admin Only Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes=50 retweets=20 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 duration=1h mute=yes Parameters: Name Type Example post_url required https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes optional 50 retweets optional 20 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 duration optional 1h mute optional yes hashtag /raid_status Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks. You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id optional value hashtag /raid_stop Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id required value hashtag /raid_history Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example limit optional value hashtag /set_raid_defaults Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior, and whether raids are enabled. These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes optional 30 retweets optional 15 replies optional 10 quotes optional 5 bookmarks optional 20 duration optional 1h mute optional no enabled optional yes hashtag /get_raid_defaults Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_message_behavior Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mode required value hashtag /get_raid_message_behavior Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_image Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message. Admin Only hashtag /auto_raid_config Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example enabled optional no duration optional 360 delay optional 5 likes optional 100 retweets optional 50 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 mute optional yes hashtag /get_auto_raid_config Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config. pages.title: Reports | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the report generation commands for X Bot. The /report command displays the most recent leaderboard report, while /recreate triggers immediate generation of a fresh report with latest X (Twitter) data, bypassing scheduled generation and consuming credits.

codeExamples.description: Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report with engagement statistics
Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report with latest X data
Generates a new report and deletes all existing report history to start fresh

useCases: Share latest community performance data immediately without waiting for scheduled report
Generate fresh report after adding new accounts or changing tracking configuration
Create on-demand reports for presentations or stakeholder meetings
Reset report history when starting a new tracking period or campaign
Verify tracking configuration is working correctly after initial setup

pages.title: Schedule Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents schedule management commands for X Bot. Users can set automated report generation schedules using daily time formats or advanced cron expressions, view current schedule configuration, and delete scheduled automation. Schedules use UTC timezone and create EventBridge rules.

Supports both simple daily time scheduling (HH:MM format) and advanced cron expressions for complex schedules. Creates an EventBridge rule for scheduled report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Copy /set_schedule 12:30 Copy /set_schedule 09:00 1 7 Copy /set_schedule 14:30 Mon Wed Fri Copy /set_schedule cron(0 12 ? * MON-FRI *) Parameters: Name Type Example schedule required value hashtag /get_schedule Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. Shows the configured UTC time or indicates if no schedule is set. Copy /get_schedule hashtag /delete_schedule Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. Deletes the EventBridge rule and database entry. Only group administrators can execute this command. pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Topic Management hashtag /set_topic Configures which forum topic (thread) the bot should send reports to in Telegram groups that use forum-style topics. If your group uses Topics (Forums), run this command inside the specific topic where you want to receive bot reports and updates. The bot will automatically detect the current topic and save it as the destination for all future reports. Useful for keeping bot reports organized in a dedicated topic instead of cluttering the main chat. Admin Only Copy /set_topic hashtag /get_topic Displays which forum topic (thread) is currently configured to receive bot reports. Shows the topic name if available, or the thread ID if the name isn't stored. pages.title: X Bot Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview page explaining X Bot's purpose as a Telegram-based X (Twitter) analytics platform providing trusted, verifiable metrics using official X API. Covers key features, performance scoring algorithm, dual-purpose platform serving projects/investors/KOLs, curation process for quality, and use cases for crypto projects, individual KOLs, and KOL teams.

codeExamples.description: Scoring formula showing how different engagement types are weighted to calculate performance points

pages.title: Platform Fees | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains the platform fee structure for using BWS blockchain services. Covers how BWS handles network fees, gas costs, and $BWS token usage. Includes pricing examples showing costs for creating NFTs and uploading to IPFS, with billing in blockchain credit fee units.
useCases: Test blockchain solutions for free on Mumbai or Sepolia test networks
Create 100 NFTs on Polygon blockchain with transparent fee structure
Upload images to IPFS with included free storage
Track monthly blockchain credit usage through billing dashboard
Execute 1000 free testnet transactions in subscription mode
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right Fees Calculator arrow-up-right-from-square API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. circle-info Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. hashtag Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. circle-check In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper arrow-up-right to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. hashtag Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK arrow-up-right to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload arrow-up-right to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. hashtag How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. circle-info BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. Previous Quick Start chevron-left Next API How Tos chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Fees Calculator Pricing Example How do I pay those Fees? Was this helpful? sun-bright desktop moon Copy 100 IPFS Images, included = 0 USD 100 NFTs x 0.02 Network Fee = 2 USD sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: We take care of everything.
Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”.

Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized.
Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience.
Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience.

In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use.
If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks.
You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free.
We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged.
Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details,
You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free.

When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit.
Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you:
Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan.
To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage.

pages.title: Quick Start | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Quick start tutorial demonstrating how to write data to blockchain in one API call. Shows step-by-step process of saving "Hello World" to Matchain blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution, including authentication, API requests, and checking job status asynchronously.

codeExamples.description: Write 'Hello World' message to Matchain blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution
JavaScript/jQuery implementation for writing data to blockchain
Fetch blockchain job status and results using the jobId from previous call

useCases: Write simple messages to blockchain without managing wallets
Save timestamped data to Matchain network
Retrieve blockchain transaction receipts and Certificate of Trust
Test blockchain integration with asynchronous job processing
Verify data integrity on public blockchain explorers

implementationSteps.title: Get API Key
implementationSteps.description: Obtain your API key for authentication. All API requests must include this key or they will return an error.

implementationSteps.title: Write Data to Blockchain
implementationSteps.description: Make a POST request to /v1/smart-contracts/call with solution set to BWS.Blockchain.Hash, specify network (matchain), operation (insert), and provide key-value parameters. Response includes jobId.

implementationSteps.title: Check Job Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the jobId to fetch results via /v1/smart-contracts/fetch. Since blockchain operations are asynchronous, poll until status shows 'completed' to receive transaction hash, URL, and Certificate of Trust.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! hashtag Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). circle-info Learn how to Get your API Key . hashtag Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash arrow-up-right solution to save a message to the blockchain. hashtag Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . API Call response hashtag Check for Status To check for job completion and results, use the jobId you got from the previous call. circle-info Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received. hashtag Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/fetch Using the previous call response jobId we fetch the blockchain job started previously. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description jobId * String (use previously returned jobId, as for example 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e) Copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your own API Key). curl Javascript Phyton You should receive a response indicating the completed job and the related blockchain receipt and certificate. Fetch API Call Response Congratulations! In just one call, you used BWS to save data into the Web3 blockchain ecosystem. implementationSteps.title: Insert Data
implementationSteps.description: Call the insert operation with a unique key and value to save data to the selected blockchain network. The operation is asynchronous and returns a jobId.

implementationSteps.title: Check Job Completion
implementationSteps.description: Use the fetch API with the provided jobId to check if the blockchain transaction has completed successfully.

implementationSteps.title: Select Data
implementationSteps.description: Retrieve previously saved data by calling the select operation with the same key used during insertion. Poll the job status using fetch API to get results.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Use the blockchain to efficiently store data in blockchain(s). BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. hashtag What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. hashtag An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. hashtag Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete API reference for BWS.IPFS.Upload operations including upload and list methods. Documents uploading JSON, images, and PDF files (under 1MB) to IPFS with base64 encoding. Shows how to retrieve IPFS CID, URIs, and list uploaded files by timestamp range.

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Content
implementationSteps.description: Encode files as base64 (for images/PDFs) or prepare JSON content. Ensure files are optimized to under 1MB size limit (hard limit 5MB).

implementationSteps.title: Upload to IPFS
implementationSteps.description: Call the 'new' operation with BWS.IPFS.Upload solution, include description and content parameters. API returns IPFS CID and URIs (ipfs:// and https://).

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.IPFS.Upload Operations Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. hashtag Upload to IPFS Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "cid" : "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "uris" : { "ipfs" : "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "url" : "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } hashtag Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string circle-exclamation Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. hashtag Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL hashtag Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. hashtag List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS hashtag List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. hashtag LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL hashtag List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file. pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page provides an overview of the BWS IPFS Upload service, which leverages the InterPlanetary File System for decentralized storage of images, PDF, and JSON files. It explains how IPFS uses content identifiers (CID) for immutable file retrieval and highlights the BWS IPFS Gateway (ipfs.bws.ninja) for accessing uploaded content.
useCases: Store NFT metadata and images for blockchain applications
Host static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects
Share structured data (JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way
Decentralized content delivery for digital assets
Ensure data integrity and provenance through IPFS
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. hashtag BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. circle-info An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T arrow-up-right hashtag Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. hashtag Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page explains NFT attributes (traits) which are specific characteristics that define each unique NFT. It provides a practical JSON example of NFT metadata for an animated cat collection, showing how traits like fur color, eye type, and accessories are structured within the attributes array to establish NFT uniqueness and value.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata structure for a digital cat collection showing how traits are defined using trait_type and value pairs

useCases: Define unique characteristics for digital art NFT collections
Create rarity-based traits to influence NFT value
Structure metadata for animated character collections
Implement trait systems for gaming NFTs
Build attribute-based filtering for NFT marketplaces
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Solution Overview chevron-right Operations chevron-right NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK NFT Attributes (traits) Making your NFT unique. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique digital assets on blockchain platforms. Those assets can define associated traits , which are specific attributes or characteristics that detail each NFT. In a practical scenario, an NFT's metadata might look something like this for a digital art collection of animated cats: Copy { "name" : "Animated Cat #001" , "description" : "A unique animated cat with distinct features." , "image" : "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" , "attributes" : [ { "trait_type" : "Fur Color" , "value" : "Black" } , { "trait_type" : "Eye Type" , "value" : "Round" } , { "trait_type" : "Accessories" , "value" : "Hat" } ] } Within the metadata, the attributes array contains the various traits of the NFT. Each trait is defined by a trait_type , which specifies the category of the trait (e.g., "Fur Color" or "Rarity"), and a value , which indicates the specific characteristic of that trait (e.g., "Black" or "Ultra-Rare"). Traits encapsulated within this metadata not only bring out the uniqueness of each token but can also influence its perceived value, especially if some traits are considered rarer than others. BWS.NFT.zK allows you to create an NFT by passing those traits . circle-exclamation Please note that it's the usage of those traits when visualizing your NFT that you must take care of and validate. For example, if you use the provided Certificate of Trust for your users' experience use case, you must check they are correctly displayed. Previous Operations chevron-left Next Blockchain Badges chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Was this helpful? sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

useCases: Mint new NFTs programmatically on blockchain networks

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: Make a POST request to the API with solution 'BWS.NFT.zK', operation 'new', and parameters including name, description, image URL/IPFS URI, and optional attributes. Receive a jobId for tracking.

implementationSteps.title: Check Transaction Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the fetch API with the provided jobId to check the blockchain transaction status and retrieve the completed NFT details including transaction hash and IPFS metadata.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer or Send NFT
implementationSteps.description: Transfer NFT ownership using either the 'transfer' operation with a wallet address or 'send' operation with an email address. Email transfers provide transfer codes for the new owner to claim ownership.

Check the blockchain transaction status and receipt using the fetch API and the provided jobId. hashtag Creates a new NFT. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "statusMessage" : "" , "info" : { "jobId" : "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } hashtag Create NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Name of your NFT. description string The NFT description. image URL (or IPFS URI) This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image. attributes optional JSON These are the attributes of your NFT. hashtag Create NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Create NFT Fetch Response chevron-right Create NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag The fetch operation , once the NFT creation job is completed, will return the following: Parameter Type Description guid string NFT unique id network string Network the NFT has been created in. nftTxHash string Blockchain transaction hash nftId string NFT id nftIPFSHash string NFT metadata file IPFS hash nftImageIPFSHash string NFT image file IPFS hash nftJson json NFT metadata json url json (check NFT Urls ) status string The NFT status: "created" or "transferred" transferCodes json (check Transfer Codes ) hashtag NFT Urls hashtag Easy-to-use URLs for you to get access to NFT's most relevant data. Parameter Type Description nft URL NFT metada URL image URL NFT image URL transaction URL NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL hashtag Transfer Codes hashtag Transfer codes that are required to transfer ownership when sending an NFT through email (check NFT Ownership to learn more). Parameter Type Description owner string Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email. receiver string The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer. hashtag List NFTs hashtag Lists your NFTs. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Get the list of your NFTs. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK operation * string list parameters JSON check Methods Parameters 200 Returns the list of your NFTs. hashtag List NFT Method Parameters circle-info If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned. Parameter Type Desciption from long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from. to long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to. hashtag List IPFS Files call Response hashtag LIST NFT Request Example cURL hashtag List NFT Response The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in. chevron-right List NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Transfer NFT circle-info This operation is asynchronous. (once executed, check the results using fetch API and the provided jobId) hashtag Transfer an NFT ownership. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string transfer parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Transfer NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. address string Wallet address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Transfer NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Transfer NFT Fetch Response The fetch operation for the NFT transfer API call will mainly state if the transfer has been successful ( completed ) and the related costs. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Send NFT by Email hashtag Send an NFT by email. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string send parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Send NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. email string Email address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Send NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Send NFT by Email Response As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag Previous Solution Overview chevron-left Next NFT Attributes (traits) chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This solution overview explains NFT ownership management in BWS.NFT.zK, covering custody, transfer options, and data location. It details how NFTs can be transferred to wallet addresses or email addresses, and explains that NFT images and metadata are always stored on IPFS with support for multiple blockchain networks including Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT creation JSON using a web-addressable URL for the image, which will be automatically uploaded to IPFS
Example NFT metadata showing an IPFS URI for the image, demonstrating how metadata is structured and stored on IPFS

useCases: Create NFTs with initial custody through BWS account before transfer
Transfer NFT ownership to users with blockchain wallets
Send NFTs to users without Web3 wallets using email addresses
Enable email recipients to later claim NFTs to their own wallets
Store NFT images and metadata permanently on IPFS

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT with Initial Custody
implementationSteps.description: Create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK which will be initially linked to your BWS account. Provide image as URL or IPFS URI, and the system will ensure both image and metadata are stored on IPFS.

implementationSteps.title: Choose Transfer Method
implementationSteps.description: Decide between transferring ownership to a blockchain wallet address (immediate transfer) or an email address (for users without Web3 wallets).

implementationSteps.title: Execute Transfer
implementationSteps.description: Use the Transfer NFT operation for wallet transfers or Send NFT operation for email transfers. Email recipients receive notification and can later transfer to their own wallet using provided transfer codes.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Solution Overview chevron-right NFT Ownership arrow-up-right-from-square NFT Data Location arrow-up-right-from-square Available Networks arrow-up-right-from-square Operations chevron-right NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Easily create NFTs. hashtag NFT Ownership When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options: Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address. Transfer ownership to an email address. circle-info Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account. hashtag Transfer NFT Ownership to a Wallet If you want to transfer an NFT ownership to another wallet address, you can simply use the Transfer NFT operation indicating the new blockchain wallet address owner. hashtag Transfer NFT Ownership to an Email Address If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address. Email received for the new NFT owner to hold and get ownership We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to. Transfer ownership page for wasy NFT transfers circle-info Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address. hashtag NFT Metadata and Image Location hashtag NFT Image Your NFT image is always hosted in IPFS. To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as: a web addressable URL, or, as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki). circle-info API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image. paragraphs: When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options:
Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address.
Transfer ownership to an email address.
Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account.
If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address.
We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to.
Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address.
To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as:
a web addressable URL, or,
as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki).
API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image.
In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required.
When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS,
